nodeMixNote: TSMC divesting VIS stake while retaining interposer outsourcing relationship, signaling continued CoWoS-L RDL interposer reliance on VIS
rationale: AMKR Q1 2026 10-Q shows capital expenditures of $224.6M in Q1 2026 vs. $79.9M in Q1 2025 (~+181% YoY), with advanced products revenue (flip chip, memory, wafer-level processing) rising to $1,372M from $1,064M (+29% YoY), reflecting accelerating investment in advanced packaging capacity; the TSM 6-K confirms TSMC retains its interposer outsourcing relationship with VIS post share-sale, but neither filing discloses CoWoS-specific lead times, utilization, or pricing.
